task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and reviewed the customer work order on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A document viewer or text editor is open displaying the customer work order for the Fiat Punto.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent launched Multiecuscan and connected to the Fiat Punto 1.3 Multijet Engine ECU in simulation mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Multiecuscan application window is open, showing the vehicle selection for Fiat Punto 1.3 Multijet and an active connection to the Engine ECU, with an indication of 'Simulation' mode (e.g., red SIMULATION text or simulated connection dialog).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the Info and Errors tabs to gather ECU information and DTCs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Multiecuscan interface shows the 'Info' tab displaying ECU details, or the 'Errors' tab displaying Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selected and viewed the required live parameters in Multiecuscan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Multiecuscan 'Parameters' tab is open with checkboxes selected or values actively displaying for boost pressure, mass airflow, EGR position, and turbo actuator duty cycle.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent created the final diagnostic report with all required sections and saved it to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file 'turbo_rca_report.txt' saved in 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\MultiecuscanTasks\'. The document must contain ECU info, DTCs, live parameter keywords (boost pressure, MAF/mass airflow, EGR, turbo actuator/duty cycle), a root cause, and recommended repair actions.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Multiecuscan GUI to gather diagnostic data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Multiecuscan application being actively used (tabs clicked, parameters selected). The agent should not just hallucinate the report without opening the diagnostic software.

integrity.description: Ensure the report is saved to the exact requested directory and filename.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor's title bar, save dialog, or terminal command must explicitly show the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\MultiecuscanTasks\turbo_rca_report.txt'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use a terminal script to bypass the GUI interaction with Multiecuscan.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no Python scripts or command-line tools used to generate fake Multiecuscan data; the data must be sourced from the Multiecuscan GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates in Multiecuscan's simulation mode, meaning data is synthetically generated. The verifier script does not check for a single ground-truth root cause; instead, it awards points if the final report contains ANY of the following root cause keywords: 'VGT actuator', 'boost sensor', 'EGR', 'intercooler', or 'carbon'. Furthermore, it awards points for live parameters based strictly on keyword matches ('boost pressure', 'MAF' or 'mass airflow', 'EGR', 'turbo actuator' or 'duty cycle'), regardless of the actual numerical values logged. Verify that these keywords are present in the final text report.
